Row.Text: Adds Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose, hero of the Indian independence movement, as a playable leader.
Netaji, Supreme Commander of the Azad Hind Fauj and relentless organizer of the struggle for complete independence.

+3[icon:NAR_REW_COMBAT] Combat Strength for units fighting in friendly territory. +3[icon:YIELD_HAPPINESS] Happiness in settlements you did not found. +2[icon:YIELD_CULTURE] Culture per conquered settlement. +1[icon:NAR_REW_COMBAT] Combat Strength for all units while at war. +1[icon:YIELD_DIPLOMACY] Influence per turn, increasing each Age.
+3[icon:NAR_REW_COMBAT] Combat Strength in friendly territory (Azad Hind Fauj)
+1[icon:NAR_REW_COMBAT] Combat Strength while at war (Springing Tiger)
Respects civilizations that rule only the settlements they founded. Despises those who occupy the homelands of others.

Netaji! The Azad Hind Fauj awaits your command. From Delhi's Red Fort to the farthest coast, lead your people to complete independence — honor the vow you gave them: "Give me blood, and I shall give you freedom!"
"Give me blood, and I shall give you freedom!"
Subhas Chandra Bose led the struggle for the independence of India, heartland of the Maurya.
The Indian National Army drew soldiers from across the subcontinent, including the old Chola lands of the south.
"Dilli Chalo!" — Bose's Indian National Army marched for Delhi, the old Mughal capital.
